one_line_definition: The decrease in ultrasonic wave speed with increasing temperature in solids, used as the basis for acoustic thermometry.
Non-invasive temperature measurement by inverting measured ultrasonic transit times using a calibrated V(T) relation.
Iterative algorithm that reconstructs internal temperature profile layer-by-layer from consecutive pulse-echo TOF differences and a calibrated V(T) curve.
Single-transducer ultrasonic measurement where the same face sends and receives reflections from internal or far-face boundaries.

aliases_seen: compressional wave velocity, V_L, P-wave velocity
one_line_definition: Speed of propagation of longitudinal (compressional) ultrasonic waves in a medium.

aliases_seen: transverse wave velocity, V_S, S-wave velocity
one_line_definition: Speed of propagation of shear (transverse) ultrasonic waves in a solid.
Elastic wave propagating along the free surface of a solid, with amplitude decaying exponentially with depth.

aliases_seen: magnetic transition, Curie point, ferromagnetic-paramagnetic transition
one_line_definition: Temperature above which a ferromagnetic material becomes paramagnetic; associated with an anomaly in ultrasonic velocity and attenuation.

aliases_seen: cementite Curie point, Fe3C Curie temperature
one_line_definition: The ~200–215°C ferromagnetic Curie temperature of the iron carbide phase Fe₃C (cementite), causing an attenuation peak in carbon steels at that temperature.
The solid-state phase transformation in steel from body-centred cubic ferrite to face-centred cubic austenite at ~910°C, causing a discontinuity in ultrasonic velocity.
The decrease in ultrasonic wave amplitude per unit path length, combining absorption and scattering losses.
Scattering of ultrasonic waves by polycrystalline grains when grain diameter D ≪ wavelength λ; attenuation ∝ f⁴·D³.
High-temperature attenuation measurement method where the transducer is on a water-cooled shoulder of the sample, keeping it at room temperature while the test section is hot.
Non-contact method using a pulsed laser to generate ultrasound thermally and an interferometer or second laser to detect surface displacement.
Non-contact ultrasonic transducer that generates and detects waves via electromagnetic induction, without couplant; limited to Curie temperature on ferromagnetic steels.
Aurivillius-phase piezoelectric ceramic with Curie temperature ~675°C, used for high-temperature ultrasonic transducers.
Film fabrication technique where a precursor solution is spin-coated and thermally pyrolysed to form a ceramic film; used to deposit BIT transducer films directly on substrates.
Piezoelectric single crystal with Curie temperature ~1150°C; used for high-temperature ultrasonic transducers, especially in 36°Y-cut for longitudinal wave generation.
Piezoelectric ceramic with Curie temperature ~570°C; used for moderate high-temperature immersion ultrasonic probes.
An acoustic waveguide (often a metal or ceramic rod) used to isolate the transducer from a hot surface by introducing a thermal and acoustic delay.
A buffer rod with a dissimilar outer cladding (e.g., steel core + stainless steel sleeve) to suppress spurious modes and extend thermal range.
A machinable glass-ceramic (Corning) used as a high-temperature acoustic waveguide; usable above 1200°C with V_L ~5900 m/s.
The piezoelectric coefficient relating electric charge developed per unit mechanical stress applied along the poling axis; key figure of merit for transducer sensitivity.
Practical upper temperature limit for a piezoelectric material, approximately half its Curie temperature (in °C), beyond which depoling becomes significant.
Reconstruction of internal property distribution (e.g., temperature, defect map) from multiple ultrasonic path integral measurements through the object.
Iterative optimisation algorithm used to solve the inverse problem of reconstructing 2D temperature fields from measured ultrasonic transit times.

aliases_seen: ray bending, Fermat's principle of least time
one_line_definition: Principle stating ultrasonic rays follow paths of minimum travel time; in non-uniform temperature fields, rays bend toward lower-velocity (lower-temperature) regions.
Characteristic linear dimension of polycrystalline grains; determines the Rayleigh scattering attenuation coefficient via α ∝ f⁴·D³.
A brittle intermetallic phase (FeCr) that forms in austenitic stainless steels above ~800°C; causes permanently elevated ultrasonic attenuation.
A lamellar microstructure of alternating ferrite and cementite; highest ultrasonic attenuation among common steel microstructures due to large effective scatterer size.
A diffusionless transformation product (BCC-tetragonal) in rapidly quenched steel; lowest attenuation, highest acoustic velocity after tempering.
An intermediate transformation product in steel formed between pearlite and martensite temperature ranges; intermediate attenuation and velocity.
Correction applied to ultrasonic transit times to account for the change in sample physical length due to thermal expansion, necessary for accurate V(T) inversion.
Methods applied in guided-wave SHM to remove the effect of temperature change on recorded signals, preventing temperature-induced false alarms.
SHM temperature compensation technique that selects from a pre-stored library the baseline signal recorded nearest to the current monitoring temperature.
SHM temperature compensation technique that stretches the time axis of a reference baseline to match velocity changes caused by temperature variation.
Ultrasonic waves propagating in thin plates or shells with dispersion characteristics dependent on frequency-thickness product; used in SHM.
Accumulation of micro-voids and micro-cracks in metals under sustained high-temperature stress; detectable by ultrasonic attenuation increase.
Reconstruction of a spatially resolved 2D temperature distribution inside or on the surface of a solid from transit-time measurements along multiple paths.
Parameter quantifying the rate of convective heat transfer between a fluid and a solid surface; characterised in Moreira 2019 review for quenching and cooling applications.
Thermodynamic parameter relating phonon frequency shift to volume change; derivable from elastic constants measured ultrasonically.
Amorphous SiO₂ used as high-temperature optical and acoustic waveguide material; has anomalous positive velocity-temperature coefficient at cryogenic temperatures.
Ultrasonic attenuation contribution arising from interaction of acoustic waves with magnetic domain walls in ferromagnetic materials; peaks near the Curie temperature.
Recursive Bayesian estimator used to smooth noisy sequential measurements; applied to ultrasonic TOF data for improved real-time temperature estimation.
A cross-linked polystyrene material with low attenuation used as an ultrasonic delay line; characterised for elevated temperature use up to 170°C.
High-performance thermoplastic polymer with better thermal stability than PMMA; characterised as an ultrasonic delay-line material at elevated temperatures.
Common transparent polymer used as ultrasonic delay line; velocity strongly temperature-dependent.
Flexible polymer with very large negative dV/dT (~2.7 m/s/°C) making it highly sensitive for ultrasonic thermometry.
A sintered ceramic with V_L ~5950 m/s at room temperature; characterised for ultrasonic thermometry applications.
High-stiffness ceramic measured by laser ultrasonics to 1600°C; has high V_L and good thermal stability.
Use of liquid gallium as a high-temperature acoustic couplant/bonding agent for attaching piezoelectric elements above the temperature limits of conventional solders.
High-temperature piezoelectric single crystal (Curie-point free to ~970°C); alternative to quartz for high-temperature SAW sensors.
The standard quartz orientation used in SAW devices; has near-zero first-order temperature coefficient of SAW velocity near room temperature.
Statistical orientation distribution of crystallites in a polycrystalline material; detectable by shear-wave birefringence in laser ultrasonic measurements.
Optical instrument using beam splitting and recombination to measure surface displacement from ultrasound; used for laser-ultrasonic detection.
High-sensitivity optical interferometer used for detecting laser-ultrasonic signals on rough hot surfaces.
